description: CORPUS-30 canonical fixture for option-bank derivation tests. Pure synthetic; no raw X content; no handles; no URLs; no post IDs. Two scenarios: rich (bike-lane civic policy) + sparse (pitch-clock sports).

sourcePost.redactedText: Cities that add a continuous bike lane reduce car traffic on the parallel arterial; the mechanism is mode shift.

candidateReplies.redactedText: Where is the primary source for the mode-shift number? Quote the page.
Quote the exact sentence the report uses to attribute traffic to bike-lane mode shift.
Counterexample: a corridor where lane addition coincided with population growth; mode shift fits less cleanly there.
Narrow the scope: this is plausible for downtown arterials, not for suburban collectors.
Rebut on logic: traffic counts can fall for population reasons, not mode shift.
Define mode shift in this scope; trips moved or trips removed?
Fair caveat: the effect may be conditional on weather and on parking pricing.

selectedDissent.redactedText: Where is the primary source for the mode-shift number? Quote the page.

playableSkeleton.targetExcerpt: Cities that add a continuous bike lane reduce car traffic on the parallel arterial
playableSkeleton.mechanism: mode-shift from car to bike on the parallel arterial
evidenceDebt: primary source for the mode-shift number
quote anchor for the broad form
playableSkeleton.antiAmplificationNote: viral civic infographics are not primary records

sourcePost.redactedText: The pitch clock made baseball better; average game length dropped twenty-four minutes.

candidateReplies.redactedText: Schedule, weather, and the bigger bases were changed the same year; causal isolation is missing.
What about a counterexample where late-inning games still drag?

selectedDissent.redactedText: Schedule, weather, and the bigger bases were changed the same year; causal isolation is missing.

playableSkeleton.targetExcerpt: average game length dropped twenty-four minutes
playableSkeleton.mechanism: causal isolation of the clock vs other rule changes
playableSkeleton.antiAmplificationNote: engagement spikes are not causal isolation
